attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who operates from distance, controlling tempo from the front rather than explosive late attacks. Finishes with well-timed sprints after terrain selection reduces group size.
team_tactics: Limited evidence of team support; appears to function independently in breakaways, using tactical awareness of GC gaps and group dynamics rather than domestique-led setups.
terrain_preference: Stages with mid-race climbs that reduce sprint competition but finish on descents or flatter roads where he can deploy his sprint. Avoids pure climbing finishes.
vulnerability: High-tempo climbing by GC teams (Trek tempo referenced) stresses him before finishes. Requires breakaway freedom; unlikely to succeed in controlled sprint scenarios.
key_patterns: Wins from small breakaway groups (6 riders), not solo or mass sprints.
Controls breakaway tempo strategically, positioning as key collaborator to manage threats.
Sprint finishing ability after climbing selection, not pure climber profile.
Operates with GC deficit (5:45 referenced), using freedom to animate stages without overall threat.
form_trajectory: positive: victory in 2022 Vuelta shows evolution from tempo worker (2018) to opportunistic winner with refined sprint timing
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 7
2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 7
